name: Failed Call to LsaRegisterLogonProcess()
description: Detects a failed attempt to call the privileged Windows function `LsaRegisterLogonProcess()`. This can indicate unauthorized attempts to interact with the Local Security Authority (LSA), often as part of a privilege escalation attempt, credential access, or a bypass of security controls. The specific failure reason `%%1794` confirms the process lacked the necessary privileges for this action.

prompts.prompt: An unauthorized attempt to interact with the Local Security Authority (LSA) has been detected on the host **{computer_name}**. The Windows Security Event Log (Event ID 4673) shows that the process **{process_name}** attempted to call the privileged function `LsaRegisterLogonProcess()` but failed due to a lack of privileges (**FailureReason: %%1794**). This is a strong indicator of a potential **privilege escalation** or **credential access** attempt (**MITRE ATT&CK T1548**). The attempt was made by the user **{subject_user_name}**. Immediate investigation is required to determine the purpose of the process and whether it is malicious or a legitimate but misconfigured application. Analyze the parent process and other recent activities of this user to assess the full scope of the threat.

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process that attempted to call the privileged service.

parameters.name: subject_user_name
parameters.description: The user account under which the process was running.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The name of the computer where the event occurred.
